g.text: Empty States Redesign — Board · List · Timeline · Recycle Bin
Principle: each empty state has a unique illustration, contextual copy, and a direct CTA — no blank columns
BOARD EMPTY STATE
[ kanban
sketch ]
Your board is a blank canvas.
Drop your first idea here — a bug to fix,
a feature to build, or a question to answer.
+ Create first ticket
or press N to start
LIST VIEW EMPTY STATE
No tickets yet.
List view shows all tickets as rows.
Start by creating your first one.
+ Add first ticket
TIMELINE EMPTY STATE
Timeline is waiting for milestones.
Create tickets with due dates and
they'll appear here as a timeline.
Perfect for sprint planning.
Go to Board → add due dates
RECYCLE BIN EMPTY STATE
[ bin ]
Nothing deleted. All tickets are alive.
Deleted tickets appear here with a 30-day restore window.
← Back to board
Design Principles for All Empty States
① Unique illustration per view — sketchy, hand-drawn style
② Warm, personal copy — not 'No data found'
Board: 'Your board is a blank canvas.'
List: 'No tickets yet.'
Timeline: 'Timeline is waiting for milestones.'
Bin: 'Nothing deleted. All tickets are alive.'
③ One clear CTA — direct, specific action
④ Faint ghost rows / dots — reinforce what the view will look like
⑤ Keyboard shortcut hint where relevant (e.g. press N)
